metadata.subject: Mathematics
metadata.description: Advance your programming skills through object-oriented design, advanced data structures, and software engineering practices. Explore complexity analysis to understand algorithm efficiency at scale. Learn how to design maintainable, extensible code and consider ethical dimensions of technology. Develop understanding of computational systems and how software powers modern society.
metadata.general_description: Master advanced programming, software design, and ethical considerations in computer science.

big_ideas.statement: Object-oriented programming enables the creation of complex, maintainable software through abstraction, encapsulation, and inheritance.
big_ideas.elaboration: By organizing code into objects with properties and methods, programmers can build systems of remarkable complexity. These principles mirror real-world organization and enable teams to collaborate effectively.

big_ideas.statement: Algorithm complexity analysis predicts how programs will perform as data grows, guiding choices about data structures and approaches.
big_ideas.elaboration: Big O notation describes how runtime grows with input size. Understanding complexity prevents building systems that collapse when real-world demands exceed expectations.

big_ideas.statement: Software design patterns provide proven solutions to common programming problems and facilitate communication among developers.
big_ideas.elaboration: Recognizing and applying design patterns improves code quality and enables programmers to understand each other's work more easily. Patterns accumulate and evolve over time.

big_ideas.statement: Computing technology shapes society in profound ways; responsible programmers must consider ethical implications of their work.
big_ideas.elaboration: Issues like privacy, bias, accessibility, and environmental impact flow from software design choices. Ethical computing requires deliberate consideration and diverse perspectives.

categories.name: Reasoning and Modelling
categories.raw: Develop software design reasoning through analysis of complexity, recognition of patterns, and thoughtful architectural decisions.

competencies.raw: Analyze algorithms and data structures for efficiency and apply design patterns to model complex systems.
i_can_statements: I can analyze the time and space complexity of algorithms using Big O notation.
I can compare data structures and select appropriate ones based on performance requirements.
I can recognize common design patterns and apply them to solve recurring design problems.
I can model complex systems through object hierarchies and relationships.

categories.name: Understanding and Solving
categories.raw: Understand advanced programming concepts and implement solutions using object-oriented design and sophisticated data structures.

competencies.raw: Implement object-oriented programs with proper encapsulation, inheritance, and polymorphism to solve complex problems.
i_can_statements: I can design and implement classes with appropriate attributes, methods, and access control.
I can create inheritance hierarchies and use polymorphism to write flexible, reusable code.
I can implement and use advanced data structures like trees, graphs, and heaps.
I can debug complex programs and optimize code for efficiency and readability.

categories.name: Communicating and Representing
categories.raw: Express software design through code documentation, UML diagrams, and explanations that enable collaboration.

competencies.raw: Communicate software design and architecture through documentation, diagrams, and clear code.
i_can_statements: I can create and interpret UML class diagrams representing object structures and relationships.
I can write comprehensive documentation explaining design decisions and usage.
I can refactor code to improve readability while maintaining functionality.
I can present software designs and explain architectural choices to other programmers.

categories.name: Connecting and Reflecting
categories.raw: Connect computational thinking to broader contexts and reflect on ethical, social, and environmental implications of computing.

competencies.raw: Reflect on the broader impacts of technology and the ethical responsibilities of software developers.
i_can_statements: I can identify and discuss ethical issues in software design including privacy, bias, and accessibility.
I can explain how computing relates to environmental sustainability and resource consumption.
I can recognize the social impact of technology choices and consider diverse perspectives.
I can apply ethical principles to make responsible decisions in software development.

areas.name: Object-Oriented Programming
areas.raw: Master the principles and practices of object-oriented design for building scalable, maintainable software.

areas.name: Advanced Data Structures
areas.raw: Work with sophisticated data structures and understand their performance characteristics.
topics: Trees: binary trees, BSTs, balanced trees, and traversal

areas.name: Algorithm Analysis and Optimization
areas.raw: Analyze algorithm efficiency and apply optimization techniques to improve performance.
topics: Big O notation and complexity classes

areas.name: Software Design and Architecture
areas.raw: Apply design patterns and architectural principles to create robust, extensible software systems.
topics: Design patterns: creational, structural, and behavioral

areas.name: Ethics and Impacts of Computing
areas.raw: Consider the broader implications of computing and develop ethical awareness as software creators.
